description: Examines the hypothesis that \gamma_2 is an exact algebraic number root of a polynomial system (as originally posited then retracted by Tiskin), mapping the failures of exact solvability to chaotic dynamics.
mathematical_formalization: P(\gamma_2) = 0 \text{ for } P \in \mathbb{Z}[x] \text{ vs } \lambda \text{ is transcendental}
analogical_connections: Similar to how the three-body problem fails to have a closed-form algebraic integral of motion, the infinite-state automaton of LCS may resist exact algebraic closure.
implementation_hypothesis: Formulate a finite algebraic relaxation using Lasserre (Sum of Squares) hierarchy. Track the degree at which the algebraic closure fails to accurately represent the infinite limit.
experiment_seed: Construct the transition polynomials for k-state approximations. Check Gröbner bases for exact rational/algebraic roots.
experimental_result: More useful for upper bounds; deferred for lower bound work.
